student_message: Two lines! Too easy. Next question!
final_message: I love your speedy energy! Let's jump right into the next challenge. Picture the big uppercase letter $$H$$ in your mind. If you wanted to fold it so the halves are perfect mirror images, how many magic fold lines could you make? Remember to check both up-and-down and side-to-side! Tell me your answer when you are ready. ✨

orchestrator_intent.goal: Provide a quiz question to test understanding.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student is highly confident and explicitly asking for the next question, making it an ideal time to assess his knowledge with a quiz.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
